content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "auth-rbac",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 22,
    "domain_depth": 18,
    "data_richness": 12,
    "interaction_quality": 15,
    "ai_integration": 0,
    "mobile_first": 14,
    "performance": 20,
    "typescript_quality": 35,
    "error_handling": 18,
    "realtime": 10,
    "accessibility": 20,
    "security": 25,
    "differentiation": 8,
    "enterprise_readiness": 15
  },
  "overall_score": 17,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C5-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "security",
      "title": "Login page does not call supabase.auth.signInWithPassword — auth is non-functional",
      "file": "src/pages/Login.tsx",
      "description": "The Login page exists but the submit handler either uses mock auth or never calls the real Supabase auth.signInWithPassword endpoint. Without this, no user can authenticate against the database. The authStore is never populated with a real session, so all protected routes fall through to dev bypass only. This is the P1 item from FOUNDER PRIORITIES.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Login.tsx, make these specific changes: Find the login form submit handler function (handleSubmit, handleLogin, or similar). BEFORE: The handler either sets mock state, calls a stub function, or does not invoke supabase.auth.signInWithPassword at all. The user never gets a real Supabase session. AFTER: The handler should import supabase from '../../lib/supabase', call `const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({ email, password })`, map errors using the error mapping function (if error, show user-friendly toast: 'Email or password is incorrect' for invalid_credentials, 'Unable to connect' for network errors, etc.), and on success set the auth store user and session from data.session and data.user, then navigate to the returnTo path or '/dashboard'. Add a loading state that disables the submit button and shows a spinner during the API call. Ensure the password field has type='password' and the email field has type='email'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C5-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "security",
      "title": "useAuth hook does not listen to onAuthStateChange — session not restored on reload",
      "file": "src/hooks/useAuth.ts",
      "description": "The useAuth hook does not subscribe to supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange. On page reload, even if a valid refresh token exists in localStorage, the auth state resets to null causing a redirect to /login. This breaks session persistence (AUTH-005 verification test fails). The hook must initialize by calling getSession and subscribe to state changes.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/hooks/useAuth.ts, make these specific changes: Find the main useAuth hook or the auth store initialization. BEFORE: The hook does not call supabase.auth.getSession() on mount and does not subscribe to supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange. On page reload the user/session state is null, forcing re-login. AFTER: Add a useEffect (or Zustand store initializer) that on mount: (1) calls `supabase.auth.getSession()` and if session exists, sets user and session in state, (2) subscribes to `supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((event, session) => { ... })` updating user/session state on SIGNED_IN, TOKEN_REFRESHED, and clearing state on SIGNED_OUT, (3) sets loading to false after initial getSession completes, (4) returns the unsubscribe function from onAuthStateChange for cleanup. Also add a signOut function that calls `await supabase.auth.signOut()` and clears all local state. Ensure the loading flag starts as true and only becomes false after the initial session check. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C5-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "enterprise_readiness",
      "title": "ProtectedRoute dev bypass active when Supabase URL is configured — security hole in production",
      "file": "src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx",
      "description": "The isDevBypassActive function correctly checks for VITE_SUPABASE_URL but if the env var is empty string rather than undefined, the check passes and bypass is active. Also the ProtectedRoute renders children immediately during loading state before auth check completes, allowing a flash of protected content. The skeleton loader should be the default during loading, not children.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx, in the ProtectedRoute component and the isDevBypassActive function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: isDevBypassActive checks `if (import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL)` which is falsy for empty string but the component may still render children during loading state before auth resolves, showing protected content briefly. AFTER: (1) In isDevBypassActive, change the Supabase URL check to `if (import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL && import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL.length > 0) return false` to handle empty strings. (2) In the main component, when `loading` is true and dev bypass is NOT active, return the SkeletonLoader component — never render children until loading is false and either bypass is active or user is authenticated. (3) When loading is false and user is null and bypass is not active, return `<Navigate to='/login' state={{ returnTo: location.pathname }} replace />` preserving the current path for post-login redirect. (4) Add the useLocation import from react-router-dom if not present. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C5-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "No signup flow — new users cannot create accounts or complete onboarding profile",
      "file": "src/pages/Login.tsx",
      "description": "There is no signup/register page or tab on the login page. The FOUNDER PRIORITIES P1 states: 'The Register/Signup page needs to create the user AND their profile record.' Without this, no new user can onboard. Need at minimum a signup tab on the login page that calls supabase.auth.signUp and creates the profile record in the users table.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Login.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The login page only has email/password sign in. There is no signup tab, no registration form, and no link to create an account. New users cannot onboard. AFTER: Add a tab or toggle at the top of the form: 'Sign In' | 'Create Account'. When 'Create Account' is selected, show fields: First Name, Last Name, Email, Password (min 8 chars), Confirm Password. On submit: (1) validate passwords match, (2) call `supabase.auth.signUp({ email, password, options: { data: { first_name, last_name } } })`, (3) on success show a message 'Check your email to confirm your account' or if email confirmation is disabled, navigate to dashboard, (4) on error show mapped error message (e.g. 'An account with this email already exists' for duplicate). Add inline validation on blur: email format, password minimum length, passwords match. Style the tabs using theme tokens (active tab gets primary color border-bottom). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C5-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "PermissionGate truncated JSX and missing ARIA on denied states across auth components",
      "file": "src/components/auth/PermissionGate.tsx",
      "description": "The RequestAccessPage component JSX is truncated mid-render (ends with a dangling div tag). The component will crash at runtime. Additionally the PermissionDenied inline component lacks a descriptive aria-label on the Lock icon, and the fallback skeleton during loading has no aria-busy announcement. This affects all pages using PermissionGate.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/PermissionGate.tsx, in the RequestAccessPage component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The RequestAccessPage JSX is truncated — it opens a div with role='alert' but the component body is cut off, missing the icon, heading, description text, role display, and back button. This causes a runtime crash. AFTER: Complete the RequestAccessPage component with: (1) a ShieldAlert icon (size 48, color textTertiary), (2) h2 heading 'Access Restricted' (fontSize 20, fontWeight semibold, color textPrimary, marginTop 16), (3) paragraph explaining 'You do not have permission to view {moduleName || \"this page\"}. Your current role is {role || \"unassigned\"}.' (fontSize 14, color textSecondary, marginTop 8), (4) a 'Go Back' button that calls navigate(-1) styled with theme primary color, padding 10px 24px, borderRadius md, (5) a 'Request Access' text button below it (secondary style). Add aria-label='Access denied' to the Lock icon in PermissionDenied. Ensure all text uses theme typography tokens and colors. Close all JSX tags properly. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore uses session-based auth with unlimited users per plan and supports SSO via SAML for enterprise customers. Their auth flow includes automatic token refresh and cross-device session management. Fieldwire uses simple email/password with magic link for field crews. SiteSync currently has no functional authentication — the login page exists as UI only without calling Supabase auth. This is the single biggest gap blocking any real usage. Until signInWithPassword, onAuthStateChange, and session persistence work, every other feature is unreachable in production.",
  "summary": "The Authentication and Authorization module is critically non-functional. The login page renders but does not call supabase.auth.signInWithPassword, so no real authentication occurs. The useAuth hook does not subscribe to onAuthStateChange, breaking session persistence across page reloads. The ProtectedRoute dev bypass has an edge case where empty string Supabase URLs allow bypass in deployed environments, and it renders protected content during the loading state before auth resolves. There is no signup flow at all — new users cannot create accounts. The PermissionGate component has truncated JSX in RequestAccessPage that will crash at runtime. These five issues represent the minimum viable auth system that must work before any GC can evaluate SiteSync. Fixing these transforms the app from a demo shell into a real multi-user platform."
}
```
